sanskrit:
व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे।
तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम्॥
transliteration:
vyāmiśreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva me |
tad ekaṁ vada niścitya yena śreyo ’ham āpnuyām ||
literal_meaning: With mixed-up words, as it were, You are bewildering my intellect; therefore, please state just one thing, having determined it, by which I may attain the highest good.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences cognitive overload when confronted with multiple valid but seemingly contradictory paths (action vs. renunciation). His request for a single, decisive directive reflects the human need for cognitive closure under existential threat.
psychological_state: Acute stress-induced analysis paralysis; executive function compromised by competing schemas
trigger_scenario: A professional simultaneously told to 'follow passion' yet 'be practical' while facing layoffs, feeling frozen between options
anchor_text: Focus on one clear path: act with devotion, letting go of outcomes.
